verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein lacking a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any documented stable surface-attachment mechanism. It is synthesized via signal-peptide-directed secretion and functions as a soluble ligand in seminal fluid and epididymal secretions. No evidence for cancer ecto-display, cell-death externalization, developmental germline-restricted membrane anchoring, lysosomal exocytosis, PM cycling, or covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. The protein equilibrates with the soluble pool and does not remain surface-anchored after washing.
